questions.question_text: Is our sell-through rate really 72% or is the data overstating it?
questions.answer_summary: An 18% overstatement in sell-through means we're making buying decisions based on phantom demand — translating to $500K+ in excess inventory risk per season.

questions.question_text: Why do we have duplicate transactions in the POS data?
questions.answer_summary: Duplicate transactions inflate revenue metrics and distort return rate calculations, creating a false picture of store performance used in expansion decisions.

questions.question_text: Which stores and departments have the worst data quality?
questions.answer_summary: Concentrating data remediation on the 5 worst-performing stores addresses 60% of data quality issues while touching only 15% of the store fleet.

questions.question_text: What's the single highest-impact fix for our reporting accuracy?
questions.answer_summary: Transaction deduplication alone would correct 15% of reported revenue variance, making P&L statements defensible without any other intervention.

questions.question_text: If the CEO asks why online and in-store numbers don't match, what do we say?
questions.answer_summary: Channel-level discrepancies suggest the omnichannel data pipeline has integration gaps — this undermines the unified commerce strategy the board approved last quarter.
